thresholdSource: criticalHigh 500: severe systemic toxicity threshold — 'concentrations greater than 500 mcg/dL are associated with severe systemic toxicity,' the level at which IV deferoxamine chelation is indicated (StatPearls NBK526131, "Iron Overload and Toxicity"). plausibleHigh 1800: above a documented case of serum iron 1799 mcg/dL after a 5x IV iron sucrose overdose that resolved without deferoxamine (PMC9772055), so a real severe-but-survivable level is held as critical, not masked as implausible. No criticalLow/plausibleLow — iron deficiency is a chronic finding, not an acute lab emergency. Draft — not clinician-approved.
